steps.text: Determine whether fuel was inspected, handled and maintained as required, since the last inspection.
Determine whether the licensee’s fuel handling procedures are adequate to perform intended functions.
Determine whether fuel is moved and inspected consistent with the requirements of the TS and the licensee’s procedures.
Determine whether fuel handling activities are conducted in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20 and the licensee’s procedures and programs for radiation protection.
Determine whether the licensee satisfied security plan requirements for fuel movement activities.
Determine whether significant fuel movement or inspection problems are identified and resolved in accordance with the licensee’s procedural controls.
Determine whether the licensee was within TS limits and met procedural requirements before resuming normal operation after fuel movement.

constraints.text: Safe fuel handling includes assurance that the fuel is not damaged, that accidental criticality does not occur, and that radiation and contamination hazards are minimized.

steps.text: Transfer fuel into a core configuration, or to racks or containers designed to prevent accidental criticality.
Calculate the minimum number of fuel bundles for a critical mass with optimum moderation, or separate the bundles using fuel racks designed for this purpose.
Monitor changes in the level of neutron flux when changing the core configuration.
Perform 1/M calculations independently by more than one person when required.
Inspect fuel and control rods in accordance with the TS and their procedures.
Check the control rods to determine their capability to continue to function.
Investigate and resolve failures in control rods to ensure they perform their intended function.
Inspect the neutron source for leakage paths if it is removed during refueling.
Inspect the installed neutron source for leakage paths such as splitting or pitting if it is removed during refueling.
Observe radiological controls during one fuel movement activity.
Review radiation protection records and discuss with responsible personnel if direct observation is not possible.
Monitor or sample for airborne radioactive particulate matter during fuel handling activities.
Perform grab sampling in accordance with approved licensee procedures to verify compliance with 10 CFR Part 20 and the TS.

constraints.text: Maintain occupational doses as low as reasonably achievable (ALARA) during fuel movement.
Inspection effort in this area is not to be duplicated in other Class I Research and Test reactor inspection procedures.

steps.text: Perform grab sampling for airborne radioactive particulate matter in accordance with approved licensee procedures.
Monitor exits from controlled areas in accordance with the 10 CFR Part 20 and the licensee's procedural requirements using a Geiger Muller (GM) detector.
Perform surveys for contamination in accordance with licensee procedures.
